summary: Matched the vi cutscene callback function fn_8031EFE4 at 100% by replacing a placeholder with a concrete HSD_GObj camera-object animation routine. The function advances a CObj animation, triggers vi_8031C9B4 events at specific animation frames, and ends the sequence by calling lb_800145F4 and gm_801A4B60 when the animation reaches its end frame. The header prototype was updated from an unknown placeholder signature to void fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj*).
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder `/// #fn_8031EFE4` in `src/melee/vi/vi0801.c` with a matched C implementation.
Added includes for `gm/gm_unsplit.h`, `lb/lb_00F9.h`, and baselib headers `aobj.h`, `cobj.h`, and `gobj.h` to support the implementation.
Implemented `fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj* gobj)` by retrieving the camera object with `GET_COBJ(gobj)` and applying `HSD_CObjAnim(cobj)`.

Added frame checks against `cobj->aobj->curr_frame`: frames `1.0F` and `30.0F` call `vi_8031C9B4(0xC, 0)`, frame `60.0F` calls `vi_8031C9B4(0x10, 0)`, and `end_frame` calls `lb_800145F4()` followed by `gm_801A4B60()`.
Updated `src/melee/vi/vi0801.h` to include `<baselib/forward.h>` and changed the prototype from `UNK_RET fn_8031EFE4(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj*)`.
The PR summary reports `fn_8031EFE4` reached 100% and `main/melee/vi/vi0801 .sdata2` improved from 0 to 38.89%.
smart_moves: Used the specific baselib camera-object type path instead of leaving the function as an unknown callback, making the signature and object access clearer.
Forward-declared HSD types in the header via `<baselib/forward.h>` while keeping full baselib includes in the C file where fields and macros are needed.
Matched float literals explicitly as `1.0F`, `30.0F`, and `60.0F`, which is important for frame comparisons and `.sdata2` matching in this codebase.
Grouped the two identical event triggers for frames 1 and 30 into a single conditional with `||`, preserving behavior while producing the matched output indicated by the PR summary.
decomp_lessons: Cutscene callback placeholders may resolve to `void` functions taking `HSD_GObj*` when they operate on camera-object animation state.
When a callback uses `GET_COBJ`, `HSD_CObjAnim`, and `cobj->aobj` fields, the implementation likely needs baselib `gobj`, `cobj`, and `aobj` includes in the C file.
Animation-frame gate logic can be expressed as direct comparisons against `aobj->curr_frame` and `aobj->end_frame`; matching may depend on using float constants with `F` suffixes.
Header cleanup should replace `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` once the real callback signature is known, and should use forward declarations when only pointer types are exposed.
naming_conventions: Kept the symbol name `fn_8031EFE4` for an address-based function whose semantic name was not established.
Preserved existing vi module naming style with address-prefixed symbols such as `vi_8031C9B4` and `un_8031F274_OnFrame`.
Used baselib type names `HSD_GObj` and `HSD_CObj` and macro-style `GET_COBJ` consistent with the surrounding engine APIs.
Header comments retain the address marker format, e.g. `/* 31EFE4 */ void fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj*);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replacing the placeholder with direct camera-object animation calls matched `fn_8031EFE4` at 100%.
Using exact float frame constants `1.0F`, `30.0F`, and `60.0F` likely contributed to the reported `.sdata2` progress for `vi0801`.
The implementation checks `curr_frame == end_frame` before calling cleanup/transition functions, matching an end-of-animation control-flow pattern.
No inline assembly or explicit nonmatching workaround is shown in the provided diff.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only human text excerpt is the PR summary reporting the matched function and `.sdata2` progress.
